chunks.text: Krste Asanović is Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School in the EECS Department at the University of California, Berkeley. He received a PhD in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 1998 then joined the faculty at MIT, receiving tenure in 2005. He returned to join the faculty at Berkeley in 2007. His main research areas are computer architecture, VLSI design, parallel programming and operating system design.
chunks.question: What is Krste Asanović's academic position and research areas?
chunks.answer: Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley in computer architecture.

chunks.text: He leads the RISC-V project at Berkeley, is Chief Architect at RISC-V International, and co-founded SiFive Inc. to support commercial use of RISC-V processors. He is also an Associate Director at the Berkeley Wireless Research Center. He received the NSF CAREER award, and is an ACM Fellow and an IEEE Fellow.
chunks.question: What are Krste Asanović's key roles and recognitions?
chunks.answer: Leads RISC-V, co-founded SiFive, ACM and IEEE Fellow.

chunks.text: Education: 1998, PhD, Computer Science, UC Berkeley. 1987, BA, Electrical and Information Sciences, University of Cambridge, UK.
chunks.question: What are Krste Asanović's educational qualifications?
chunks.answer: PhD from UC Berkeley, BA from Cambridge.

chunks.text: Research Areas: Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), Integrated Circuits (INC), Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), Design, Modeling and Analysis (DMA). Research Centers: Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC), SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE).
chunks.question: What are Krste Asanović's research areas and centers?
chunks.answer: Computer architecture, integrated circuits, operating systems, design.

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: DAC's Most Influential Paper Award (2025), Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) Fellow (2018), Institute of Electrical & Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Fellow (2014), Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science (2014), ACM Distinguished Scientist Award (2011), Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching of Computer Science (2010), Okawa Research Grant (2007), NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) (2001).
chunks.question: What major awards has Krste Asanović received?
chunks.answer: ACM Fellow, IEEE Fellow, NSF CAREER award.
